metadata.title: SAM3 Batch Size 1 + Gradient Accumulation + OOM Robustification
metadata.author: Petteri Teikari + Claude Code
metadata.estimated-cost: Relaunch: ~$4-8 (16 SAM3 conditions on L4 spot + 2 zero-shot baselines)

rule-compliance.rule: Debug run = full production: only batch_size and grad_accum change, all factors identical
Zero hardcoded parameters: batch_size and grad_accum come from config, not code defaults
Zero improvisation on configs: changes explicitly authorized by user
No standalone scripts: all training through Prefect flows in Docker
Explicit Docker volume mounts: no /tmp for artifacts

phase.name: Config Changes
phase.description: Add model-specific batch_size and gradient_accumulation_steps overrides to factorial configs. The global fixed.batch_size remains as the default for models without an override. SAM3 variants get BS=1 + accum=4.

task.title: Add model_overrides section to factorial configs
task.description:
Add a new top-level `model_overrides` key to debug.yaml, paper_full.yaml, and smoke_test.yaml. Each entry maps a model_family name to its specific batch_size and gradient_accumulation_steps. Models not listed use the global fixed.batch_size with gradient_accumulation_steps=1 (default).
Config structure: ```yaml model_overrides: sam3_topolora: batch_size: 1 gradient_accumulation_steps: 4 sam3_hybrid: batch_size: 1 gradient_accumulation_steps: 4 sam3_vanilla: batch_size: 1 gradient_accumulation_steps: 1 ```
DynUNet and MambaVesselNet are NOT listed because they use the global fixed.batch_size=2 with no gradient accumulation. Explicit is better than implicit, but listing them would be noise since they match defaults.

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_factorial_model_overrides_present() that loads each factorial YAML via yaml.safe_load() and asserts model_overrides exists with sam3_topolora.batch_size=1 and sam3_topolora.gradient_accumulation_steps=4. Write test: test_model_overrides_consistent_across_configs() that verifies all factorial YAMLs have identical model_overrides (Rule 27: debug = production).
tdd-approach.green: Add the model_overrides section to each YAML file.

verification.command: uv run python -c "import yaml, pathlib; cfg = yaml.safe_load(pathlib.Path('configs/factorial/debug.yaml').read_text(encoding='utf-8')); assert cfg['model_overrides']['sam3_topolora']['batch_size'] == 1"

task.title: Wire model_overrides reading in run_factorial.sh
task.description:
Modify run_factorial.sh to read model_overrides from the factorial YAML for the current model being launched. For each condition in the launch loop:
1. Look up model_overrides.{model}.batch_size (default: fixed.batch_size) 2. Look up model_overrides.{model}.gradient_accumulation_steps (default: 1) 3. Pass as --env BATCH_SIZE={value} --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS={value} to sky jobs launch
Implementation: Add a Python helper function call inside the launch loop that reads the override for the current model. Use the existing Python YAML parsing pattern already in the script (no new dependencies).
The BATCH_SIZE env var overrides the global fixed.batch_size. The GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS env var is new (default 1 if not set).

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_run_factorial_dry_run_model_overrides() that runs run_factorial.sh --dry-run with a test config containing model_overrides and verifies the dry-run output shows BATCH_SIZE=1 for SAM3 models and BATCH_SIZE=2 for DynUNet.
tdd-approach.green: Add Python inline to resolve per-model batch_size and grad_accum inside the launch loop. Add --env BATCH_SIZE and --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS to the sky jobs launch command.

task.title: Wire gradient_accumulation_steps in train_flow.py training loop
task.description:
Modify the training loop in train_flow.py to support gradient accumulation:
1. Read GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS from environment (via resolve function, not os.environ.get with fallback) 2. In the training loop: a. Divide loss by grad_accum_steps before backward() b. Call optimizer.step() + optimizer.zero_grad() every grad_accum_steps iterations c. Adjust epoch iteration count for learning rate schedulers 3. Log effective_batch_size = batch_size * grad_accum_steps to MLflow
IMPORTANT: This must work with MONAI SupervisedTrainer. If SupervisedTrainer does not natively support gradient accumulation, implement it via the iteration_update event handler or switch to a manual training loop with MONAI transforms.
Meta's Issue #200 bug (collate_fn_api_with_chunking) does NOT apply to standard PyTorch grad accum. Our MONAI loop uses standard DataLoader.

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_gradient_accumulation_divides_loss() that mocks the training loop with grad_accum_steps=4, verifies loss is divided by 4, and optimizer.step() is called every 4th iteration. Write test: test_gradient_accumulation_effective_batch_logged() that verifies effective_batch_size is logged to MLflow params. Write test: test_gradient_accumulation_steps_1_is_noop() that verifies grad_accum_steps=1 produces identical behavior to the current loop.
tdd-approach.green: Implement gradient accumulation in the training loop.

task.title: Verify gradient accumulation works with MONAI SupervisedTrainer
task.description:
MONAI's SupervisedTrainer may or may not natively support gradient accumulation. This task verifies compatibility and documents the integration approach:
1. Check if SupervisedTrainer has a gradient_accumulation parameter 2. If yes: use it directly (library-first, Rule #3) 3. If no: implement via iteration_update event or manual loop 4. Write an integration test that runs 8 iterations with accum=4 and verifies optimizer.step() was called exactly 2 times
Web-search MONAI docs if needed (Rule #10: verify beyond knowledge cutoff).

tdd-approach.red: Write integration test: test_monai_trainer_gradient_accumulation() that creates a tiny DynUNet, runs 8 training iterations with accum=4, and asserts weight updates happened exactly twice (at iterations 4 and 8).
tdd-approach.green: Wire gradient accumulation into the MONAI training loop, using the library-native approach if available.

phase.name: Code Robustification
phase.description: Add OOM detection, pre-training VRAM estimation, and model-specific VRAM profiles so that future OOM failures are caught early with actionable diagnostics rather than opaque CUDA errors.

task.title: Add OOM detection and diagnostics in train_flow.py
task.description:
Wrap the training loop in a try/except that catches torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError (PyTorch 2.x) and RuntimeError with "CUDA out of memory" (legacy). On OOM:
1. Log current VRAM usage via torch.cuda.memory_summary() 2. Log the model family, batch_size, gradient_accumulation_steps 3. Log a diagnostic message suggesting batch_size reduction 4. Log to MLflow as a FAILED run with OOM tag 5. Re-raise the exception (Rule #25: loud failures, never silent discard)
This ensures that OOM failures produce actionable logs rather than raw CUDA stack traces, making the next debugging pass faster.

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_oom_detection_logs_diagnostics() that mocks torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError during training, verifies the diagnostic message includes model_family, batch_size, and VRAM usage. Write test: test_oom_detection_reraises() that verifies OOM is re-raised after logging (Rule #25).
tdd-approach.green: Add try/except block around the training loop with structured OOM diagnostics.

task.title: Add pre-training VRAM estimate check
task.description:
Before starting training, compute an estimated VRAM requirement based on: - Model profile VRAM data (configs/model_profiles/*.yaml) - Current batch_size - Available GPU VRAM (torch.cuda.get_device_properties)
If estimated VRAM exceeds 90% of available VRAM, log a WARNING with the estimate and suggest reducing batch_size. If estimated VRAM exceeds 100%, raise RuntimeError (fail loudly before wasting spot instance time).
This check complements sam3_vram_check.py (which only checks minimum VRAM for the model family, not batch-size-specific estimates).

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_vram_estimate_sam3_topolora_bs2_exceeds_l4() that mocks L4 GPU (24 GB) and verifies the estimator raises RuntimeError for sam3_topolora at batch_size=2. Write test: test_vram_estimate_sam3_topolora_bs1_fits_l4() that verifies sam3_topolora at batch_size=1 passes the check. Write test: test_vram_estimate_dynunet_bs2_fits_l4() that verifies dynunet at batch_size=2 passes the check.
tdd-approach.green: Implement VRAM estimator that reads model profiles and computes batch-size-scaled estimates.

task.title: Update model profile YAMLs with batch-size-specific VRAM data
task.description:
Update configs/model_profiles/sam3_topolora.yaml and sam3_hybrid.yaml with the measured VRAM data from the 8th pass and RunPod smoke tests:
sam3_topolora.yaml: vram.training_gb: 21.9 (at BS=2, OOM on L4) vram.measured_config.batch_size: 2 Add: vram.per_batch_size.1: 13.0 (estimated) Add: vram.per_batch_size.2: 21.9 (measured, OOM)
sam3_hybrid.yaml: vram.training_gb: 7.2 (at BS=1, OK) Add: vram.per_batch_size.1: 7.2 (measured) Add: vram.per_batch_size.2: 14.4 (estimated)
mambavesselnet.yaml: vram.training_gb: 6.7 (at BS=2, measured from 8th pass) vram.measured: true vram.measured_gpu: "L4" vram.measured_date: "2026-03-24"
IMPORTANT (Rule #31): These are data updates from measurements, not structural schema changes. The vram.per_batch_size sub-key is new but was explicitly authorized by the user in this planning session.

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_model_profiles_have_measured_vram() that loads all model profiles and verifies sam3_topolora, sam3_hybrid, dynunet, and mambavesselnet all have vram.measured=true with non-null training_gb.
tdd-approach.green: Update the YAML files with measured VRAM data.

task.title: Update sam3_vram_check.py to account for batch_size
task.description:
The current sam3_vram_check.py enforces a flat 16 GB minimum for training mode, regardless of batch size. Update it to:
1. Accept an optional batch_size parameter (default 1) 2. For batch_size > 1, scale the minimum VRAM requirement: estimated = base_vram + (batch_size - 1) * per_sample_activation_cost 3. Read per_sample_activation_cost from the model profile YAML if available 4. Log the batch-size-adjusted estimate in the error message
This makes the VRAM check actionable: instead of "needs >= 16 GB" it says "sam3_topolora at batch_size=2 needs ~22 GB, you have 24 GB (margin: 2 GB, DANGEROUS — reduce batch_size to 1)."

tdd-approach.red: Write test: test_vram_check_batch_size_2_warns_on_l4() that mocks L4 GPU and verifies check_sam3_vram(variant="sam3_topolora", mode="training", batch_size=2) raises RuntimeError. Write test: test_vram_check_batch_size_1_passes_on_l4() that verifies batch_size=1 passes on L4. Write test: test_vram_check_backward_compatible() that verifies calling check_sam3_vram() without batch_size still works (default=1).
tdd-approach.green: Add batch_size parameter to check_sam3_vram() with scaling logic.

phase.name: Relaunch
phase.description:
Relaunch failed SAM3 conditions with corrected batch_size=1 and gradient_accumulation_steps=4. DynUNet and MambaVesselNet are NOT relaunched (16/16 SUCCEEDED). Zero-shot baselines are launched for the first time.
IMPORTANT: Relaunch requires explicit user authorization (Rule: no-unauthorized-infra). This plan documents WHAT to launch; the user decides WHEN.

task.title: Relaunch SAM3 TopoLoRA (8 conditions) at BS=1, accum=4
task.description:
Relaunch all 8 SAM3 TopoLoRA training conditions: 4 losses x 2 aux_calibration x 1 fold = 8 GPU jobs
Each job runs with: --env BATCH_SIZE=1 --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS=4 --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_topolora
Expected VRAM: ~13 GB (well within L4's 24 GB) Expected cost: ~$2-4 (8 jobs on L4 spot, 2 epochs each for debug)
Use: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --resume configs/factorial/debug.yaml The --resume flag will skip SUCCEEDED DynUNet/MambaVesselNet jobs and re-submit FAILED SAM3 TopoLoRA jobs with the new config.

tdd-approach.green: Launch via run_factorial.sh --resume with updated config

task.title: Relaunch SAM3 Hybrid (8 conditions) at BS=1, accum=4
task.description:
Relaunch all 8 SAM3 Hybrid training conditions: 4 losses x 2 aux_calibration x 1 fold = 8 GPU jobs
Each job runs with: --env BATCH_SIZE=1 --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS=4 --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_hybrid
Expected VRAM: ~7.2 GB (well within L4's 24 GB) Expected cost: ~$2-4 (8 jobs on L4 spot, 2 epochs each for debug)

tdd-approach.green: Launch via run_factorial.sh --resume with updated config

task.title: Verify SAM3 Vanilla at BS=1
task.description:
SAM3 Vanilla is a zero-shot baseline (frozen encoder evaluation), not part of the training factorial grid. Verify it works at BS=1 by checking:
1. If sam3_vanilla already SUCCEEDED in the 8th pass zero-shot launches 2. If not, launch via the zero-shot baseline section of run_factorial.sh 3. Expected VRAM: ~3.5 GB (verified on RTX 4090 at BS=1)
Note: SAM3 Vanilla uses frozen encoder (no backward pass through ViT-32L), so batch_size=1 is its natural operating point regardless of VRAM.

tdd-approach.green: Verify via sky jobs queue or launch if needed

task.title: Launch zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla + vesselfm)
task.description:
Launch zero-shot baselines as defined in debug.yaml:
1. sam3_vanilla: frozen_encoder_eval on MiniVess, 1 fold 2. vesselfm: zero_shot_only on DeepVess, 1 fold
These are NOT part of the factorial Cartesian product. They are separate jobs launched after the trainable conditions complete.
The run_factorial.sh script handles these automatically via the zero_shot_baselines section at the bottom of the launch loop.

task.title: Monitor and verify all conditions SUCCEEDED
task.description:
After all jobs are launched, monitor until completion:
1. sky jobs queue — check for RUNNING / SUCCEEDED / FAILED 2. For any FAILED jobs: sky jobs logs JOB_ID to diagnose 3. Target: 32/32 trainable conditions SUCCEEDED + 2/2 zero-shot SUCCEEDED 4. Verify MLflow has all 34 runs logged with correct parameters
Expected timeline: - Debug (2 epochs): ~10-20 min per job on L4 - With spot preemption + queue time: ~1-3 hours total wall clock
Success criteria: - 8/8 DynUNet SUCCEEDED (already done) - 8/8 MambaVesselNet SUCCEEDED (already done) - 8/8 SAM3 TopoLoRA SUCCEEDED (new, BS=1) - 8/8 SAM3 Hybrid SUCCEEDED (new, BS=1) - 1/1 SAM3 Vanilla zero-shot SUCCEEDED - 1/1 VesselFM zero-shot SUCCEEDED - Total: 34/34
